proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.4pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Regime stability flagged as shifting (day 1 in Bear); if regime reverts to Choppy quickly, cohort deactivates without contributing edge
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8 — pending review may revise sizing rules
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +41.4pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +41.4pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Current regime is Bear (day 1, shifting) so the cohort is now active, but portfolio Bear exposure is 0/28 while the 100% concentration trigger is on Choppy — promotion does not compound the flagged concentration, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-18) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +28.2pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
n=17 is near the lower bound for Tier M; cohort tier could drift on next archive comparison if 1-2 losses are added
Sample concentration risk: with only 17 trades, cannot rule out clustering on a few Bear days — recommend monitoring for non-cluster-day reinforcement as regime persists
R-multiple 0.98 is borderline and under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance); realized edge may compress if forced exits dominate
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +28.2pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 well above Tier M floor, +28.2pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Regime just shifted to Bear (day 1) so the cohort is now active, but current portfolio Bear exposure is 0/28 while the 100% concentration trigger is on Choppy — promotion of a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY cohort does not compound flagged concentration and may actually diversify. No prior decisions on this specific BULL_PROXY×Bear cohort; adjacent UP_TRI×Bear cohort has continuous approval history with no trader rejection.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type×sector = Choppy regime (100%) with UP_TRI×Choppy×Pharma cell at 28.57% (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type×sector = Choppy regime (100%) with UP_TRI×Choppy×Pharma cell at 28.57%; recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI×sector=Pharma until Pharma share drops below 20% via natural Day-6 exits; additionally block new UP_TRI adds while regime=Choppy until Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes from Candidate to Tier M
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind the entire 28-position long book simultaneously with no cohort-level edge justification; Pharma-specific shock (regulatory/USFDA) would hit 8 positions concurrently with no offsetting short exposure

evidence: regime_concentration triggered: Choppy at observed=1.0 vs 0.80 threshold (28/28 positions)
UP_TRI signal share at 27/28 = 96.4%, above 0.85 threshold; book is fully directional long
Matched cohort regime=Choppy__signal_type=UP_TRI is Candidate tier (n=234) — edge unvalidated
Secondary cells UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank (17.86%) and UP_TRI×Choppy×Chem (14.29%) compound sector clustering
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.concentration_value: Choppy regime (100%) with UP_TRI×Choppy×Pharma cell at 28.57%
decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI×sector=Pharma until Pharma share drops below 20% via natural Day-6 exits; additionally block new UP_TRI adds while regime=Choppy until Choppy×UP_TRI cohort promotes from Candidate to Tier M
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would unwind the entire 28-position long book simultaneously with no cohort-level edge justification; Pharma-specific shock (regulatory/USFDA) would hit 8 positions concurrently with no offsetting short exposure
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (observed 1.0, above 0.80 threshold) AND UP_TRI×Choppy×Pharma cell breaches the 0.20 cell threshold at 28.57% (8 of 28 positions). UP_TRI also dominates signal mix at 27/28 = 96.4% (above 0.85 threshold) with the book fully directional long. Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=234) — unvalidated edge across all concentration cells.
